summary: Tune the Data Lifecycle Manager policies that automate EBS snapshot creation and retention for tagged volumes: retention, schedule timing, enable/disable, adding schedules or tags, and retiring a policy.

operations.title: Change snapshot retention count
operations.summary: Set how many automated snapshots are kept per volume before the oldest is deleted. More snapshots means higher storage cost.
operations.description: Adjusts how many automated snapshots DLM keeps per volume before rotating out the oldest; raising this increases EBS snapshot storage cost.

params.name: policy
params.label: DLM policy
params.help: The lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Name of the schedule block within the policy (policies usually have one schedule).

params.name: new_count
params.label: New retention count

bounds.semantic: number of snapshots retained per volume

params.help: How many snapshots to keep before the oldest is deleted (AWS allows 1-1000).

operations.title: Change snapshot schedule time and interval
operations.summary: Change when and how often automated snapshots run, for example moving them outside a busy batch-job window.
operations.description: Retimes when the automated snapshot window runs and how often, e.g. shifting the nightly snapshot out of the batch-job window or moving from 24h to 12h intervals.

params.name: policy
params.label: DLM policy
params.help: The lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Name of the schedule block within the policy.

params.name: new_interval
params.label: New interval (hours)
params.help: How often snapshots are taken; AWS only permits these interval values.

params.name: new_times
params.label: New start time (UTC, on the hour)
params.help: DLM only supports on-the-hour start times, e.g. 03:00Z.

operations.title: Enable or disable a DLM policy
operations.summary: Turn this backup policy on or off. Turning it off immediately stops new automated snapshots until it's re-enabled.
operations.description: Switches the policy between ENABLED and DISABLED; disabling immediately stops all future automated snapshots for volumes it covers, so existing snapshots are unaffected but backup coverage gaps until re-enabled.

params.name: policy
params.label: DLM policy
params.help: The lifecycle policy to enable or disable.

params.name: new_state
params.label: New state
params.help: DISABLED stops the policy from creating or deleting snapshots until re-enabled.

operations.title: Add a new snapshot schedule to a policy
operations.summary: Add a new snapshot schedule to this policy. Applying recreates the whole policy under a new ID, and an engineer reviews it first.
operations.description: Adds an additional schedule block to an existing policy, e.g. a weekly long-retention schedule alongside the daily one, without disturbing the current schedule.

params.name: policy
params.label: DLM policy
params.help: The lifecycle policy to add a schedule to.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: New schedule name
params.help: Descriptive name for the new schedule, e.g. 'Weekly Long Retention'.

params.name: new_interval
params.label: Interval (hours)
params.help: How often this schedule runs.

params.name: interval_unit
params.label: Interval unit
params.help: DLM interval-style schedules are always specified in hours.

params.name: new_times
params.label: Start time (UTC, on the hour)
params.help: DLM only supports on-the-hour start times, written as "HH:00" with no trailing Z (e.g. "03:00").

params.name: new_count
params.label: Retention count

bounds.semantic: number of snapshots retained per volume

params.help: How many snapshots this schedule keeps before rotating out the oldest.

params.name: copy_tags
params.label: Copy source volume tags
params.help: Whether snapshots inherit tags from the source EBS volume.

operations.title: Add a tag to a DLM policy
operations.summary: Add a tag to this policy for cost tracking or ownership. Metadata only, it doesn't change snapshot behavior.
operations.description: Adds a key/value tag to the policy resource for cost allocation or ownership tracking; does not affect snapshot behaviour.

params.name: policy
params.label: DLM policy
params.help: The lifecycle policy to tag.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: Tag key, e.g. CostCentre or Environment.

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: Tag value, e.g. prod-app.

operations.title: Delete a DLM lifecycle policy
operations.summary: Permanently delete this backup policy; can't be undone. Volumes lose automated snapshots unless replaced. An engineer reviews first.
operations.description: Permanently removes the policy so DLM stops managing snapshots for the volumes it covers; existing snapshots already taken are not deleted, but no new automated snapshots or retention rotation will occur, so this must not be done for a volume without a replacement backup arrangement.

params.name: policy
params.label: DLM policy
params.help: The lifecycle policy to delete.

params.name: confirm_no_replacement
params.label: Confirm no replacement backup policy exists
params.help: Must be confirmed true to proceed; ensures the volumes covered will not be left without automated backups.

operations.title: Edit policy description
operations.summary: Edit the free-text description on this backup policy. Cosmetic only, no effect on snapshots.
operations.description: Update the free-text description of a DLM lifecycle policy. Updates in place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy whose description you are editing, chosen from Terraform-managed policies so the text change targets a known policy.

params.name: description
params.label: Policy description
params.help: Up to 500 chars. DLM allows only [0-9A-Za-z _.-].

operations.title: Set snapshot create interval
operations.summary: Set how many days between automated snapshots, from 1 to 7. Existing snapshots are unaffected.
operations.description: Change how often snapshots are created (policy_details.create_interval), in days. Bounded 1-7, updates in place, reversible, no downtime. Does not delete existing snapshots.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy whose snapshot creation frequency you are changing, selected from Terraform inventory; existing snapshots are not affected.

params.name: create_interval
params.label: Create interval (days)
params.help: Snapshot creation frequency in days. AWS allows 1-7.

operations.title: Set snapshot retain interval
operations.summary: Set how many days snapshots are kept, from 2 to 14. Shortening this can delete snapshots older than the new window.
operations.description: Change how long snapshots are retained (policy_details.retain_interval), in days. Bounded 2-14, updates in place. Reducing this can prune existing snapshots, so it is guardrailed.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy whose snapshot retention you are changing, selected from Terraform-managed policies so this guardrailed change (shortening retention can prune snapshots) applies only to a known policy.

params.name: retain_interval
params.label: Retain interval (days)
params.help: Snapshot retention in days. AWS allows 2-14. Lowering this may delete snapshots already past the new window.

operations.title: Set target resource types
operations.summary: Choose whether this policy backs up volumes, instances, or both. Changes which resources get automated snapshots.
operations.description: Change which resource types the policy targets (policy_details.resource_types). Enum allowlist VOLUME/INSTANCE, updates in place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy whose target resource types you are changing, picked from Terraform inventory so the retargeting applies to a known policy.

params.name: resource_types
params.label: Resource types
params.help: Which resource type this EBS snapshot policy manages.

operations.title: Set target resource locations
operations.summary: Choose where the resources this policy covers are located: the main Region, a Local Zone, or an Outpost.
operations.description: Change where the policy's target resources live (policy_details.resource_locations). Enum allowlist CLOUD/LOCAL_ZONE/OUTPOST, updates in place, reversible, no downtime.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy whose target resource locations you are changing, picked from Terraform inventory so the retargeting applies to a known policy.

params.name: resource_locations
params.label: Resource locations
params.help: Where the targeted resources are located.

operations.title: Change execution IAM role
operations.summary: Change which IAM role this policy uses to create and manage snapshots. A permissions change, so it's guardrailed.
operations.description: Point the policy at a different IAM role that DLM assumes to manage snapshots (execution_role_arn). Bounded to existing roles via inventory picker, updates in place, reversible, no downtime. Guardrailed because it is a permissions change.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy whose execution role you are re-pointing, chosen from Terraform-managed policies so this permissions change applies only to a known policy.

params.name: execution_role_arn
params.label: Execution IAM role
params.help: Existing IAM role DLM will assume to create and manage snapshots.

operations.title: Remove a tag
operations.summary: Remove one tag from this policy. Metadata only, and reversible by adding the tag back.
operations.description: Remove a single key from the policy's tags map. Updates in place, reversible (re-add the tag), no downtime.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to remove the tag from, selected from Terraform inventory so only a managed policy's metadata changes.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key to remove
params.help: Key of the tag to remove from the policy.

operations.title: Request another change to DLM Lifecycle Policy
operations.summary: Request any other change to this backup policy that isn't covered by a specific form. An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.
operations.description: Catch-all: request any DLM lifecycle policy change not offered as a bounded self-service form. An engineer authors and reviews the Terraform.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The lifecycle policy to change, chosen from live inventory.

params.name: purpose
params.label: What change do you need?
params.help: Anything not offered as a bounded form — an engineer authors the Terraform.

operations.title: Toggle default-policy tag copying
operations.summary: Turn on or off whether a default policy copies the source's tags onto its snapshots or AMIs. Cosmetic only.
operations.description: Set policy_details.copy_tags — whether a default policy copies tags from the source volume/instance onto the created snapshot or AMI. Cosmetic tag inheritance; doc default false.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: copy_tags
params.label: Copy source tags to snapshots/AMIs
params.help: true copies source-resource tags onto created snapshots/AMIs. Doc default false.

operations.title: Set retention extension on source deletion
operations.summary: Choose whether snapshots and AMIs stay protected if the source or this policy is deleted. Turning it off removes a safety net.
operations.description: Set policy_details.extend_deletion — whether a default policy extends snapshot/AMI retention when the source volume/instance is deleted or the policy enters error/disabled/deleted state. Turning it off removes a retention safety net; doc default false.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: extend_deletion
params.label: Extend retention on source deletion
params.help: true keeps snapshots/AMIs retained if the source or policy is deleted/disabled.

operations.title: Set default policy kind (VOLUME/INSTANCE)
operations.summary: Set whether this account-wide default policy covers volumes or instances. Applies broadly, so review carefully.
operations.description: Set the top-level default_policy argument — the type of AWS-managed default policy (VOLUME or INSTANCE) that applies account-wide without tag targeting. Broad blast radius.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: default_policy
params.label: Default policy kind
params.help: Type of default policy: VOLUME or INSTANCE.

operations.title: Set default-policy resource type
operations.summary: Choose whether this default policy's snapshots target volumes or instances. Changes which resources are covered.
operations.description: Set policy_details.resource_type (singular) — whether a default policy targets VOLUME or INSTANCE resources. Moderate coverage-scope blast radius.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: resource_type
params.label: Default-policy resource type
params.help: Resource type a default policy targets.

operations.title: Set policy language (SIMPLIFIED/STANDARD)
operations.summary: Switch this policy between a simplified default style and a fully custom one. This can break the current setup.
operations.description: Set policy_details.policy_language — SIMPLIFIED for a default policy, STANDARD for a custom policy. Switching restructures which other arguments apply and can break the live configuration.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: policy_language
params.label: Policy language
params.help: SIMPLIFIED = default policy, STANDARD = custom policy.

operations.title: Set policy type
operations.summary: Change what kind of thing this policy manages: EBS snapshots, machine images, or event-triggered actions.
operations.description: Set policy_details.policy_type — EBS_SNAPSHOT_MANAGEMENT, IMAGE_MANAGEMENT, or EVENT_BASED_POLICY. Changes what the policy manages entirely; broad blast radius. Doc default EBS_SNAPSHOT_MANAGEMENT.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: policy_type
params.label: Policy type
params.help: What the policy manages: EBS snapshots, EBS-backed AMIs, or event-based actions.

operations.title: Toggle root-volume exclusion (CreateSnapshots)
operations.summary: Choose whether the root volume is skipped when this policy snapshots a multi-volume instance.
operations.description: Set policy_details.parameters.exclude_boot_volume — exclude the root volume from multi-volume instance snapshots created via CreateSnapshots. Narrow capture-scope toggle; doc default false.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: exclude_boot_volume
params.label: Exclude root volume
params.help: true excludes the root volume from CreateSnapshots-based snapshots. Doc default false.

operations.title: Toggle instance reboot on AMI capture
operations.summary: Choose whether instances are rebooted when this policy captures an image. Allowing a reboot causes a brief disruption each run.
operations.description: Set policy_details.parameters.no_reboot — true means targeted instances are NOT rebooted when an AMI policy runs; false reboots them (brief instance disruption at run time). Doc default true.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: no_reboot
params.label: Do not reboot instances
params.help: true = instances not rebooted on AMI capture (default). false reboots instances at each policy run.

operations.title: Toggle boot-volume exclusion (default policy)
operations.summary: Choose whether boot volumes are excluded from this default policy's backup coverage.
operations.description: Set policy_details.exclusions.exclude_boot_volumes — exclude volumes attached as an instance boot volume from a default policy's coverage. Narrow capture-scope toggle; doc default false.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: exclude_boot_volumes
params.label: Exclude boot volumes
params.help: true excludes instance boot volumes from a default policy. Doc default false.

operations.title: Add a volume/instance exclusion tag
operations.summary: Add a tag that excludes matching volumes or instances from this default policy's backups. Can silently change what's covered.
operations.description: Add one key/value entry to policy_details.exclusions.exclude_tags — a default policy excludes resources carrying this tag. Changing exclusions can silently drop or add volumes from backup coverage.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Exclusion tag key
params.help: Tag key identifying resources to exclude.

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Exclusion tag value
params.help: Tag value identifying resources to exclude.

operations.title: Add a target tag
operations.summary: Add a tag that determines which volumes or instances this policy backs up. Changes what's covered.
operations.description: Add one key/value entry to policy_details.target_tags — resources matching resource_types and tagged with ANY target tag are backed up. Adjusting targeting changes which volumes are covered.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Target tag key
params.help: Tag key resources must carry to be targeted.

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Target tag value
params.help: Tag value resources must carry to be targeted.

operations.title: Set event action name
operations.summary: Rename the action label on this event-based policy. Cosmetic only, no functional effect.
operations.description: Set policy_details.action.name — the descriptive label for an event-based policy's action. Cosmetic, no functional effect.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: action_name
params.label: Action name
params.help: Descriptive label for the event action (safe charset).

operations.title: Toggle event cross-region copy encryption
operations.summary: Turn on encryption for a cross-region snapshot copy made when the source wasn't encrypted. A security change reviewed by an engineer.
operations.description: Set policy_details.action.cross_region_copy.encryption_configuration.encrypted — enable encryption for an unencrypted-source cross-region copy made by an event-based action. Direct security boundary.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: encrypted
params.label: Encrypt copied snapshot
params.help: true encrypts an unencrypted-source cross-region copy.

operations.title: Set event cross-region copy KMS key
operations.summary: Choose the encryption key used for event-triggered cross-region snapshot copies. A security-sensitive change.
operations.description: Set policy_details.action.cross_region_copy.encryption_configuration.cmk_arn — the KMS key encrypting event-based cross-region snapshot copies. Encryption-key security boundary.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: cmk_arn
params.label: KMS key
params.help: Existing KMS key used to encrypt the copied snapshot.

operations.title: Set event cross-region copy retention
operations.summary: Set how long event-triggered cross-region snapshot copies are kept. Shortening this permanently deletes copies sooner.
operations.description: Set policy_details.action.cross_region_copy.retain_rule interval and interval_unit — how long event-based cross-region snapshot copies are retained before permanent deletion. Shrinking permanently deletes copies sooner. Max 100 years.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: interval_unit
params.label: Retention unit
params.help: Unit of time for the retention interval.

params.name: interval
params.label: Retention interval

bounds.semantic: <=100 years: 36500 days / 5200 weeks / 1200 months / 100 years depending on unit

params.help: Retention amount; effective max is 100 years in the chosen unit.

operations.title: Set event-source trusted snapshot owners
operations.summary: Choose which other accounts' shared snapshots can trigger this policy. A trust-boundary change, reviewed by an engineer.
operations.description: Set policy_details.event_source.parameters.snapshot_owner — the list of AWS account IDs whose snapshot-sharing can trigger the event-based policy. Direct trust boundary.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: account_ids
params.label: Trusted AWS account IDs

bounds.semantic: each entry is a 12-digit AWS account ID

params.help: 12-digit AWS account IDs permitted to trigger this policy.

operations.title: Set snapshot creation location
operations.summary: Choose where scheduled snapshots are kept: in the same Region, or on the same Outpost.
operations.description: Set policy_details.schedule.create_rule.location — CLOUD (same Region as source) or OUTPOST_LOCAL (same Outpost). Affects data-residency/durability posture. Doc default CLOUD.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Name of the schedule block to edit (policies usually have one schedule).

params.name: location
params.label: Creation location
params.help: Where snapshots are created.

operations.title: Set pre/post script SSM handler
operations.summary: Choose the AWS-managed script that runs before or after each snapshot; it executes commands on the instance, so an engineer reviews it.
operations.description: Set policy_details.schedule.create_rule.scripts.execution_handler to a documented AWS-managed SSM document — one of the values offered in the allowlist. Command-execution surface; custom SSM documents are not offered here and route to the engineer catch-all.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Name of the schedule block to edit.

params.name: execution_handler
params.label: Script handler (managed SSM document)
params.help: AWS-managed handler: VSS backups or application-consistent snapshots.

operations.title: Set pre/post script stages
operations.summary: Choose whether the pre-snapshot script, post-snapshot script, or both run. Skipping one can downgrade snapshots to crash-consistent.
operations.description: Set policy_details.schedule.create_rule.scripts.stages — which script phases DLM runs (PRE, POST, or both). Disabling a stage can downgrade an application-consistent snapshot to crash-consistent. Doc default both.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Name of the schedule block to edit.

params.name: stages
params.label: Script stages
params.help: Which script phases DLM runs on target instances.

operations.title: Toggle crash-consistent fallback on script failure
operations.summary: Choose whether a crash-consistent snapshot is still taken if the pre-snapshot script fails. Turning this off risks a failed snapshot.
operations.description: Set policy_details.schedule.create_rule.scripts.execute_operation_on_script_failure — whether DLM falls back to a crash-consistent snapshot if the pre-script fails. Disabling risks a failed/incomplete snapshot. Doc default true.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Name of the schedule block to edit.

params.name: execute_operation_on_script_failure
params.label: Fall back to crash-consistent snapshot
params.help: true (default) takes a crash-consistent snapshot if the pre-script fails.

operations.title: Set script retry count
operations.summary: Set how many times a failed pre or post snapshot script is retried, from 0 to 3 attempts.
operations.description: Set policy_details.schedule.create_rule.scripts.maximum_retry_count — number of times DLM retries failed pre/post scripts. Integer 0-3, doc default 0.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Name of the schedule block to edit.

params.name: maximum_retry_count
params.label: Maximum retry count
params.help: Retries for failed scripts; integer 0-3 (doc default 0).

operations.title: Add a static snapshot/AMI tag
operations.summary: Add a fixed tag applied to every snapshot or AMI this schedule creates. Cosmetic only.
operations.description: Add one key/value entry to policy_details.schedule.tags_to_add — extra static tags applied to snapshots/AMIs created by this schedule. Cosmetic.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Name of the schedule block to edit.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Tag key
params.help: Tag key applied to created snapshots/AMIs.

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Tag value
params.help: Tag value applied to created snapshots/AMIs.

operations.title: Add a variable snapshot tag
operations.summary: Add a tag whose value fills in automatically, such as the instance ID or run timestamp, when the snapshot is created.
operations.description: Add one key/value entry to policy_details.schedule.variable_tags — dynamic tags whose value resolves at execution time. Value must be $(instance-id) or $(timestamp); usable only when resource_types is INSTANCE.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Name of the schedule block to edit.

params.name: tag_key
params.label: Variable tag key
params.help: Tag key for the dynamic value.

params.name: tag_value
params.label: Variable value
params.help: Only $(instance-id) or $(timestamp) are valid values.

operations.title: Set time-based snapshot retention
operations.summary: Set how long this schedule's snapshots are kept, by time instead of count. Shortening it permanently deletes older ones sooner.
operations.description: Set policy_details.schedule.retain_rule interval and interval_unit — time-based retention for the schedule's snapshots (alternative to count). Shrinking permanently deletes older snapshots sooner. Max 100 years.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Name of the schedule block to edit.

params.name: interval_unit
params.label: Retention unit
params.help: Unit of time for time-based retention.

params.name: interval
params.label: Retention interval

bounds.semantic: <=100 years: 36500 days / 5200 weeks / 1200 months / 100 years depending on unit

params.help: Retention amount; effective max is 100 years in the chosen unit.

operations.title: Set AMI deprecation count
operations.summary: Set how many of the oldest AMIs get marked deprecated. Deprecation only hides them, it doesn't delete anything.
operations.description: Set policy_details.schedule.deprecate_rule.count — number of oldest AMIs to deprecate. Deprecation hides (does not delete) AMIs. Integer 1-1000.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Name of the schedule block to edit.

params.name: count
params.label: AMIs to deprecate
params.help: Number of oldest AMIs to deprecate; integer 1-1000.

operations.title: Set AMI deprecation interval (days)
operations.summary: Set how many days after creation an AMI is marked deprecated. Deprecation only hides it, it doesn't delete anything.
operations.description: Set policy_details.schedule.deprecate_rule interval and interval_unit — time after which AMIs created by this schedule are deprecated. This op sets a day-based interval (1-36500 days = up to 100 years); other units use the engineer catch-all. Deprecation hides, not deletes.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Name of the schedule block to edit.

params.name: interval_unit
params.label: Deprecation unit
params.help: Fixed to DAYS so the day count is precisely bounded.

params.name: interval
params.label: Deprecation interval (days)
params.help: Days after which AMIs are deprecated; 1-36500.

operations.title: Set fast-restore snapshot count
operations.summary: Set how many of the newest snapshots get fast restore enabled. More snapshots with this on costs more.
operations.description: Set policy_details.schedule.fast_restore_rule.count — number of snapshots to enable with fast snapshot restore. Cost/performance implication. Integer 1-1000.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Name of the schedule block to edit.

params.name: count
params.label: Snapshots with fast restore
params.help: Number of snapshots enabled with fast restore; integer 1-1000.

operations.title: Set fast-restore duration (days)
operations.summary: Set how many days fast restore stays turned on for each snapshot. Longer durations cost more.
operations.description: Set policy_details.schedule.fast_restore_rule interval and interval_unit — how long fast snapshot restore stays enabled on created snapshots. Day-based (1-36500 days); other units use the engineer catch-all. Cost/performance implication.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Name of the schedule block to edit.

params.name: interval_unit
params.label: Duration unit
params.help: Fixed to DAYS so the day count is precisely bounded.

params.name: interval
params.label: Fast-restore duration (days)
params.help: Days fast restore stays enabled; 1-36500.

operations.title: Set snapshot sharing target accounts
operations.summary: Choose which other AWS accounts this schedule's snapshots are shared with. A data-exposure change reviewed by an engineer.
operations.description: Set policy_details.schedule.share_rule.target_accounts — the list of AWS account IDs the schedule's snapshots are shared with. Direct data-exposure/security boundary.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Name of the schedule block to edit.

params.name: account_ids
params.label: Share-with AWS account IDs

bounds.semantic: each entry is a 12-digit AWS account ID

params.help: 12-digit AWS account IDs to share snapshots with.

operations.title: Set archive-tier snapshot count
operations.summary: Set how many archived snapshots are kept per volume in the low-cost archive tier. Lowering this permanently deletes older ones.
operations.description: Set policy_details.schedule.archive_rule.archive_retain_rule.retention_archive_tier.count — max snapshots retained in the EBS Snapshots Archive tier per volume. Lowering permanently deletes older archived snapshots. Integer 1-1000.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Name of the schedule block to edit.

params.name: count
params.label: Archived snapshots per volume
params.help: Max snapshots kept in the archive tier per volume; integer 1-1000.

operations.title: Set archive-tier retention interval
operations.summary: Set how long snapshots stay in the low-cost archive tier before permanent deletion. Shortening this deletes archived ones sooner.
operations.description: Set policy_details.schedule.archive_rule.archive_retain_rule.retention_archive_tier interval and interval_unit — how long snapshots stay in the archive tier before permanent deletion. Shrinking permanently deletes archived snapshots sooner. Max 100 years.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Name of the schedule block to edit.

params.name: interval_unit
params.label: Archive retention unit
params.help: Unit of time for archive-tier retention.

params.name: interval
params.label: Archive retention interval

bounds.semantic: <=100 years: 36500 days / 5200 weeks / 1200 months / 100 years depending on unit

params.help: Archive-tier retention amount; effective max is 100 years in the chosen unit.

operations.title: Toggle cross-region copy encryption
operations.summary: Turn on encryption for a cross-region snapshot copy when the source wasn't encrypted. A security change reviewed by an engineer.
operations.description: Set policy_details.schedule.cross_region_copy_rule.encrypted — enable encryption for an unencrypted-source cross-region copy. Security boundary. When the schedule has multiple cross-region copy rules the engineer targets the correct one.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Name of the schedule block to edit.

params.name: encrypted
params.label: Encrypt cross-region copy
params.help: true encrypts an unencrypted-source cross-region copy.

operations.title: Set cross-region copy KMS key
operations.summary: Choose the encryption key used for cross-region snapshot copies. A security-sensitive change handled by an engineer.
operations.description: Set policy_details.schedule.cross_region_copy_rule.cmk_arn — the KMS key encrypting cross-region snapshot copies. Encryption-key security boundary. When the schedule has multiple cross-region copy rules the engineer targets the correct one.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Name of the schedule block to edit.

params.name: cmk_arn
params.label: KMS key
params.help: Existing KMS key used to encrypt cross-region copies.

operations.title: Set cross-region copy retention
operations.summary: Set how long cross-region snapshot copies are kept. Shortening this permanently deletes copies sooner, and can't be undone.
operations.description: Set policy_details.schedule.cross_region_copy_rule.retain_rule interval and interval_unit — how long cross-region snapshot copies are retained before permanent deletion. Shrinking permanently deletes copies sooner. Max 100 years.

params.name: policy
params.label: Lifecycle policy
params.help: The DLM lifecycle policy to modify.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Name of the schedule block to edit.

params.name: interval_unit
params.label: Retention unit
params.help: Unit of time for the retention interval.

params.name: interval
params.label: Retention interval

bounds.semantic: <=100 years: 36500 days / 5200 weeks / 1200 months / 100 years depending on unit

params.help: Retention amount; effective max is 100 years in the chosen unit.

operations.title: Provision a new DLM lifecycle policy
operations.summary: Request a brand-new EBS snapshot policy; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new Data Lifecycle Manager policy as a complete specification: which volumes it targets, its snapshot schedule, and retention. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: policy_name
params.label: Policy name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes. Names the policy in the generated draft.

params.name: description
params.label: Policy description
params.help: Up to 500 chars. DLM allows only [0-9A-Za-z _.-].

params.name: execution_role
params.label: Execution IAM role
params.help: Existing IAM role DLM will assume to create and manage snapshots.

params.name: state
params.label: Initial state
params.help: DISABLED stops the policy from creating or deleting snapshots until it is turned on.

params.name: resource_types
params.label: Target resource type
params.help: Which resource type this EBS snapshot policy manages.

params.name: target_tags_note
params.label: Target tags
params.help: Which existing tag key/value on the estate's volumes this policy should match — an engineer fills this in from the estate's tagging convention.

params.name: schedule_name
params.label: Schedule name
params.help: Policies hold schedules; start with one.

params.name: new_interval
params.label: Interval (hours)
params.help: How often this schedule runs.

params.name: interval_unit
params.label: Interval unit
params.help: DLM interval-style schedules are always specified in hours — written into the draft, never asked.

params.name: new_times
params.label: Start time (UTC, on the hour)
params.help: DLM only supports on-the-hour start times, written as "HH:00" with no trailing Z (e.g. "03:00").

params.name: retention_count
params.label: Retention count

bounds.semantic: number of snapshots retained per volume

params.help: How many snapshots to keep before the oldest is deleted.

params.name: copy_tags
params.label: Copy source volume tags
params.help: Whether snapshots inherit tags from the source EBS volume.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Policy name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this policy protects
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this policy.

decisions: The attribute block(s) declaring the target-tag key/value pair are an engineer decision, matched to the estate's existing tagging convention
Image-management and event-based policy types are an engineer decision — this form covers the common EBS-snapshot-management case
Cross-region copy, exclusions, and a second schedule are engineer follow-ups after creation
